# BitLogin

> A static, relay-backed portable Nostr account protocol. A familiar login
> name and password (or an existing `nsec`) unlocks a portable Nostr identity
> stored as encrypted "capsules" on public Nostr relays — no account server,
> no password-reset email, no database. Ships as an embeddable custom element
> plus a NIP-07-compatible `window.nostr` provider that any static site can
> drop in with one script tag.

## Integrate in one snippet

```html
<script type="module" src="https://bitlogin.vercel.app/vendor/bitlogin/bitlogin.js"></script>
<bitlogin-auth
  vault-relays="wss://relay-one.example,wss://relay-two.example,wss://relay-three.example"
  discovery-relays="wss://discovery-one.example,wss://discovery-two.example">
</bitlogin-auth>
```

`bitlogin.js` and `cryptoWorker.js` (plus the shared chunk they both import)
must be deployed side by side — the main script resolves the worker's URL at
runtime relative to its own location, so this works from any path. For
production, self-host these files rather than depending on the demo
deployment above.

## What it gives your app

- A `<bitlogin-auth>` custom element (Shadow DOM, no CSS collisions) handling
  create account, sign in, forgot-password recovery via a 12-word phrase, and
  password rotation.
- A `window.nostr` provider shaped like the de facto NIP-07 interface
  (`getPublicKey`, `signEvent`, `getRelays`, `nip44.encrypt`/`decrypt`) —
  existing Nostr app code that already calls `window.nostr` works unmodified,
  same as it would against a browser extension (Alby, nos2x, …).
- `window.bitlogin` — installed as soon as the script loads, independent of
  login state: `{ version, isActiveSigner() }`. Use
  `window.bitlogin.isActiveSigner()` to tell whether BitLogin specifically
  (not another extension) currently backs `window.nostr`.
- Events on the element: `bitlogin-login` (`detail.publicKey`) and
  `bitlogin-logout`.
- Private keys never leave a dedicated Web Worker; the main thread only ever
  sees public keys, signed events, and ciphertext.

## Key facts for integration

- No build step required to consume it — plain `<script type="module">`.
- Don't give the element `id="bitlogin"` — browsers auto-expose elements with
  a matching `id`/`name` as a same-named global, which shadows
  `window.bitlogin`. Use `id="bitlogin-widget"` or similar.
- Configure relays via the `vault-relays` and `discovery-relays` attributes
  (comma-separated `wss://` URLs); omit to use BitLogin's small built-in
  bootstrap list.
- Manual (user-chosen) passwords are opt-in and discouraged by default — the
  widget generates a high-entropy passphrase and recommends it, since a
  downloadable encrypted capsule can be guessed against offline forever.
- Recovery is a 12-word BIP-39-style phrase, independent of the password;
  losing the password never locks the user out if they still have the
  phrase.

## Reference docs

- [Integration guide](/docs.html) — full walkthrough with code samples.
- [Live account manager demo](/account.html) — create/login/recover, sign a
  test event, rotate password, export identity.
- [Protocol specification](https://github.com/PR0M3TH3AN/bitlogin/blob/main/docs/spec.md) —
  the full BitLogin v0.3 protocol (crypto construction, capsule formats,
  relay architecture, threat model).
- [Second-factor / recovery options design notes](https://github.com/PR0M3TH3AN/bitlogin/blob/main/docs/second-factor.md)
  and [future recovery-path options](https://github.com/PR0M3TH3AN/bitlogin/blob/main/docs/future-recovery-options.md) —
  proposals, not all implemented; each notes its own status.
- [Source repository](https://github.com/PR0M3TH3AN/bitlogin) — monorepo:
  `packages/core` (protocol implementation), `packages/widget` (the embeddable
  element + provider), `packages/demo` (this site).

## Status

Experimental alpha. Verified with an in-memory mock-relay test suite and
real-browser end-to-end runs, but not independently audited. See the README
in the source repository for the full implemented/deferred feature list.
